_provenance: Claude-authored original passages (DeepSeek not used). Gaokao 七选五 style: passage with 5 gaps, 7 full-sentence options (5 correct + 2 distractors), options are topic/transition sentences, answers = blank-order indices into the 7 options.

sets.passage: Starting at a new school can feel frightening. You may worry that you will never fit in or find people who understand you. (1)____ With a little courage and patience, you can build real friendships in just a few weeks. First, be open and approachable. Smile, say hello, and show that you are happy to meet new people. (2)____ A simple question such as 'Which class are you in?' can easily start a conversation. Second, join clubs or sports teams that interest you. (3)____ When you share an activity you enjoy, talking to others feels natural rather than forced. Third, be a good listener. (4)____ People feel valued when someone truly pays attention to what they say. Finally, give friendships time to grow. (5)____ Real trust is built slowly, through small moments of kindness repeated day after day.
bank: However, the truth is that almost everyone feels nervous at first.
Do not be afraid to make the first move.
Shared interests are one of the fastest ways to connect with others.
Instead of talking only about yourself, ask questions and pay attention to the answers.
Do not expect to become best friends overnight.
Teachers usually give much less homework during the first week.
Eating a good breakfast every morning improves your memory.
sets.explain_zh: (1) 承接'担心融不进去'用转折 0；(2) 承接'微笑问好'、引出'主动提问' 1；(3) 承接'加入兴趣社团' 2；(4) 承接'做好的倾听者' 3；(5) 承接'给友谊时间生长' 4。干扰项 5/6 与段落主题无关。

sets.passage: Every year, millions of tons of good food are thrown away. Much of this waste happens not in restaurants or shops, but in ordinary homes. (1)____ The good news is that a few simple habits can make a real difference. Start by planning your meals before you shop. (2)____ When you know exactly what you need, you are far less likely to buy food that will rot at the back of the fridge. Next, learn to store food correctly. (3)____ For example, keeping bread in a cool, dry place helps it stay fresh for longer. Another useful idea is to be creative with leftovers. (4)____ Yesterday's vegetables can become today's soup, and stale bread can be turned into a tasty snack. Finally, do not judge food only by its appearance. (5)____ A slightly odd-shaped carrot or a spotted banana is usually perfectly safe and delicious.
bank: This means we all share responsibility for the problem, and for the solution.
Making a shopping list is one of the easiest ways to avoid buying too much.
Different foods need different conditions to stay fresh.
With a little imagination, almost any leftover can become a new meal.
Many fruits and vegetables are wasted simply because they look imperfect.
Restaurants in big cities often throw away large amounts of unsold meals.
Cooking together with friends is a fun way to spend the weekend.
sets.explain_zh: (1) 承接'浪费发生在普通家庭'→'我们都有责任' 0；(2) 承接'购物前规划'→购物清单 1；(3) 承接'正确储存'→不同食物不同条件 2；(4) 承接'巧用剩菜'→发挥想象 3；(5) 承接'不要只看外表'→因卖相被浪费 4。干扰项 5/6 偏题。
